title: Call me a n00b but
wikitext:
[]: Call me a n00b, but...             Quote Post by OdetoJoy » Mon Feb 14, 2005 8:34 pm Er, this probably has been discussed a bazillion times before, but do any of you realize the animation of the eyes in EoE is much different then the series? Also, they dont show the eyes many times throughout the movie. For example, Shinji's shadow of his hair covers his hair. I don't know if this had anything to do with Production I.G.'s animation, but that is what I expected. Woot for the second post.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Feb 14, 2005 11:00 pm There are a lot of peculiar differences in designs in EOE. Being that there were a different crew of animators and designers, some of the designs got a little.... weird. Here's an excellent breakdown by our esteemed Reichu: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=131495

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 15, 2005 9:15 am The Eva Monkey wrote: There are a lot of peculiar differences in designs in EOE. Being that there were a different crew of animators and designers, some of the designs got a little.... weird.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Fri Feb 18, 2005 10:25 pm Not to mention that more money/time/effort was put into the movie. So I'm a little confused as to why a different Unit-02 design was made just for the movie, but whatever. I like the way she looks in EoE... looks more ready to kick ass and such. Truthfully, I never really noticed how different Unit-02 looked only because I was so engrossed in the cool action. ._.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Feb 20, 2005 6:57 pm Reichu wrote: I was under the impression that the main animators for EoE had worked on the TV series, as well... Takeshi Honda -- who changed the Evas' look for the movie -- for instance.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 20, 2005 7:37 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Whatever the case, it was not the exact same group of people doing the exact same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Sun Feb 20, 2005 7:37 pm Ayanami wrote: Not to mention that more money/time/effort was put into the movie. So I'm a little confused as to why a different Unit-02 design was made just for the movie, but whatever. I like the way she looks in EoE... looks more ready to kick ass and such. Truthfully, I never really noticed how different Unit-02 looked only because I was so engrossed in the cool action. ._.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Sun Feb 20, 2005 8:57 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: There are a lot of peculiar differences in designs in EOE. Being that there were a different crew of animators and designers, some of the designs got a little.... weird. Here's an excellent breakdown by our esteemed Reichu: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=131495

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Feb 21, 2005 8:49 pm I'm a bit surprised that Kyoko's change of... self doesn't seem to get as much Obsession Press as do so many other minute details. I've never heard anyone try to "justify" the change in terms of its place in the plot; it just gets written off as, "Oh, it's just something they did."

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Aug 21, 2009 2:43 am The Eva Monkey wrote: There are a lot of peculiar differences in designs in EOE. Being that there were a different crew of animators and designers, some of the designs got a little.... weird. Here's an excellent breakdown by our esteemed Reichu: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=131495

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri Aug 21, 2009 5:53 am As I understand this sort of thing, it has more to do with purpose/goals than with the individual team working on it. The TV series was for TV, so they sort of went along with the usual weird hair colours and whatnot. EoE was a theatrical release and super-dark in tone, so they avoided super-deformed humour sequences and got rid of unnatural hair and eye colours (unless the character was, uh, unnatural to begin with). It isn't unlike the way you write a novel, and how an author might write certain scenes differently depending on the mood. Language might be exaggerated for effect. It is sort of like how girls with yellow-orange hair are called "redhead" even though it clearly isn't red. Depending on how exaggerated the author is being at any one time, they might go more intimate and describe the actual colour, or they could be light-hearted and use such notions as "redhead". That is my earth-shaking analysis!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Aug 21, 2009 7:36 am The first page has been archived, but that seems to be about it. http://web.archive.org/web/20070619202153/http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=131495

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Aug 21, 2009 10:43 am Honestly, these animation changes have never bothered me (nor the continuity errors). Hell, I'd love to see an anime that features different artists for different episodes and create a style similar to that of the Sandman comic series where designs changed depending on the artist.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Aug 21, 2009 11:52 am I think the biggest reason for the slight changes in look were due to the heftier budget

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Aug 21, 2009 6:42 pm Ok, archived: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7479 Some of the images weren't salvageable, so bug Reichu about fixing them

[]:             Quote Post by armoredcorecc » Sat Aug 22, 2009 7:38 am Well I always thought that the battle between the MP Evas and Eva 02 was amazing but still not as detailed as the series animation. At times the Eva-02 looks much less detailed, as one example of that is that the shoulder units are shorter than normal, and then in the next scene they might be normal sized again, just to go back to being short. Don't get me wrong, it was amazing, especially for a 1990s movie, but the animation just wasn't what the series was.
